content.divergence_summary: The same grey-brown squirrel appeared at the east boundary with the same hazelnut and the same open posture on both paths. The encounter lasted under a minute either way. What differed was whether the offer could be read in time — main was operating thin on attention and the calculation didn't complete before the grey squirrel left; alt1-on-time was resourced and sharp and the trade executed cleanly. One path ends the day with an untouched boundary and a small gnaw of something unresolved. The other ends with a hazelnut in the cache that came from somewhere west — the first object in the territory that arrived through exchange rather than foraging.
content.branch_a_path: The offer registered but didn't convert. YY saw the changed posture, understood the category, started the math — and ran out of attention before the math finished. The grey squirrel waited forty seconds and left. The east line held; nothing material was lost. The west spot produced again in the afternoon. The day closed intact, with the boundary slightly more complicated than it was in the morning.
content.branch_b_path: The offer converted on first read. YY pulled a surplus pine nut from the east cache, brought it to the boundary, and the exchange happened in under a minute. The hazelnut's shell texture implied western stores YY hasn't mapped. The boundary is now also a channel. The cache has its first received item.
key_differences: main: no trade, no inventory; alt1-on-time: first inventory item acquired (hazelnut, grey squirrel's west stores)
main: attention 0.55→0.50 (incomplete calculation); alt1-on-time: attention 0.82→0.80 (clean read and execution)
main: east boundary unchanged in function; alt1-on-time: east boundary is now both a territorial line and a trade channel
alt1-on-time: goal updated to prioritize west territory based on hazelnut intelligence; main: goal unchanged
shared_elements: same grey-brown squirrel, same hazelnut, same open posture at the same east boundary
neither path involved aggression or territorial loss
west foraging productive in main; the west as a direction is relevant on both paths
